scenario: Soft lockup or Hard lockup due to race between ring_buffer_detach() and ring_buffer_wakeup()
symptoms: NMI watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#0 stuck for 22s!
Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 23
Kernel panic - not syncing: softlockup: hung tasks

workflow.thought: Check system information and panic details
workflow.observation: System BIOS, vendor, and product details

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of the panicked task
workflow.observation: Stack trace showing generic_exec_single+0xfa and IPI waiting

workflow.thought: Check backtrace of CPU 1 to see why IPI not handled
workflow.observation: CPU 1 stuck in perf_event_wakeup() with interrupts disabled

workflow.thought: Disassemble __wake_up to understand panic location
workflow.observation: Panic at pop %r14 instruction after spin_unlock_irqrestore

workflow.thought: Filter backtrace to show exception context
workflow.observation: Exception RIP at __wake_up+0x49 with interrupts disabled

workflow.thought: Disassemble irq_work_run to understand execution flow
workflow.observation: irq_work_run calling irq_work_run_list with interrupts disabled

workflow.thought: Check system information for lockup details
workflow.action: sys | grep -i -e load -e node -e rel -e date -e panic -e cpus
workflow.observation: System load, release, panic message, and CPU count

workflow.thought: Search log for lockup messages
workflow.observation: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP messages

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of CPU 42 hard lockup
workflow.observation: Stack showing perf_event_wakeup+0x45 in NMI exception

workflow.thought: Evaluate RFLAGS value to understand interrupt state
workflow.observation: Binary flags showing interrupt status bits

workflow.thought: Disassemble perf_pending_event to see call chain
workflow.observation: perf_pending_event calling perf_event_wakeup

workflow.thought: Disassemble perf_event_wakeup function header
workflow.observation: Function prologue with register pushes

workflow.thought: Calculate and read stack frame to find perf_event
workflow.observation: Address calculation for stack frame

workflow.thought: Read memory at calculated stack address
workflow.observation: perf_event address ffff88206563d000

workflow.thought: Examine perf_event.pending field offset
workflow.observation: pending field at offset ffff88206563d2c0

workflow.thought: Inspect irq_work structure at pending address
workflow.observation: irq_work with func pointing to perf_pending_event

workflow.thought: Check perf_event.waitq field offset
workflow.observation: waitq field at offset ffff88206563d290

workflow.thought: Examine wait queue status
workflow.observation: Wait queue is empty

workflow.thought: Examine ring_buffer.event_list for empty list
workflow.observation: event_list next and prev pointers are NULL

workflow.thought: Inspect wait_queue_head_t structure
workflow.observation: wait queue lock and task_list pointers

workflow.thought: Check perf_event.waitq field with offset and hex
workflow.observation: waitq field matches wait_queue_head_t address

workflow.thought: Examine perf_event.rb_entry list pointers
workflow.observation: rb_entry points to itself, not initial rb

root_cause_analysis: Below commit will fix this issue https://lore.kernel.org/patchwork/patch/465937/
